# Lutra

> Lutra is a general-purpose, statically typed query language for querying data and expressing data structures. It compiles to SQL and can run on DuckDB, PostgreSQL, and a reference interpreter.

Lutra preserves type information between different software components. Programs are built from expressions, pipelines make data flow easy to read, and the same program can target different execution backends.

The project is in early development, created by Aljaž M. Eržen and available under the Apache 2.0 license.

- Source code: https://codeberg.org/lutra/lutra

## Usage

- [Usage overview](https://lutra-lang.org/docs/usage/): Getting started with Lutra.
- [Command line guide](https://lutra-lang.org/docs/usage/cli/): Install the CLI and run, check, and compile Lutra programs.
- [Rust guide](https://lutra-lang.org/docs/usage/rust/): Use Lutra from Rust.
- [Python guide](https://lutra-lang.org/docs/usage/python/): Use Lutra from Python.

## Learn

- [Introduction](https://lutra-lang.org/docs/learn/introduction/): Core ideas behind Lutra — types, expressions, pipelines, and backends.
- [Basics](https://lutra-lang.org/docs/learn/basics/): Constants, functions, types, and basic expressions.
- [Pipelines](https://lutra-lang.org/docs/learn/pipelines/): How pipelines chain operations on data.
- [Tabular data](https://lutra-lang.org/docs/learn/tabular-data/): Working with tables and rows.
- [Aggregations](https://lutra-lang.org/docs/learn/aggregations/): Grouping and aggregating data.
- [Date and time](https://lutra-lang.org/docs/learn/date-time/): Date and time types and operations.
- [Reporting](https://lutra-lang.org/docs/learn/reporting/): Building reports from query results.
- [Projects](https://lutra-lang.org/docs/learn/projects/): Organizing Lutra code into projects.

## Language Reference

- [Syntax](https://lutra-lang.org/docs/reference/language/syntax/): Lutra syntax overview.
- [Literals](https://lutra-lang.org/docs/reference/language/literals/): Literal values for numbers, text, dates, and more.
- [Types](https://lutra-lang.org/docs/reference/language/types/): The type system — primitives, tuples, arrays, and custom types.
- [Expressions](https://lutra-lang.org/docs/reference/language/expressions/): Expression forms and evaluation.
- [Patterns](https://lutra-lang.org/docs/reference/language/patterns/): Pattern matching.
- [Definitions](https://lutra-lang.org/docs/reference/language/definitions/): Defining constants, functions, and types.
- [Modules](https://lutra-lang.org/docs/reference/language/modules/): Module system and imports.
- [Operators](https://lutra-lang.org/docs/reference/language/operators/): Built-in operators.

## Standard Library

- [`std`](https://lutra-lang.org/docs/reference/language/std/): Standard library overview.
- [`std::option`](https://lutra-lang.org/docs/reference/language/std/option/): Nullable values.
- [`std::math`](https://lutra-lang.org/docs/reference/language/std/math/): Mathematical operations.
- [`std::text`](https://lutra-lang.org/docs/reference/language/std/text/): Text functions.
- [`std::fs`](https://lutra-lang.org/docs/reference/language/std/fs/): File system operations.
- [`std::sql`](https://lutra-lang.org/docs/reference/language/std/sql/): SQL interface.
- [`std::date`](https://lutra-lang.org/docs/reference/language/std/date/): Date operations.
- [`std::time`](https://lutra-lang.org/docs/reference/language/std/time/): Time-of-day operations.
- [`std::timestamp`](https://lutra-lang.org/docs/reference/language/std/timestamp/): Timestamp operations.

## Runtime

- [Runner model](https://lutra-lang.org/docs/reference/runtime/runner-model/): How Lutra executes programs across backends.
- [CLI reference](https://lutra-lang.org/docs/reference/runtime/cli/): All commands and options.
- [DuckDB](https://lutra-lang.org/docs/reference/runtime/duckdb/): Running Lutra on DuckDB.

## Internals

- [Binary format](https://lutra-lang.org/docs/reference/internals/binary-format/): Lutra's compiled binary format.
- [Format spec](https://lutra-lang.org/docs/reference/internals/format-spec/): Detailed format specification.
- [Type representations](https://lutra-lang.org/docs/reference/internals/representations/): How types are represented internally.

## About

- [Status](https://lutra-lang.org/docs/about/status/): Project status and roadmap.
- [Motivation](https://lutra-lang.org/docs/about/motivation/): Why Lutra was created.
- [Design decisions](https://lutra-lang.org/docs/about/design/): Key design choices.
- [Relation to PRQL](https://lutra-lang.org/docs/about/prql/): How Lutra relates to PRQL.
